name: Christmas
description: Festive traditions, songs, films, and holiday lore

questions.q: Which country is traditionally credited with starting the custom of decorating Christmas trees?

source.notes: 16th-century Germany. Prince Albert (German) popularized it in Britain in the 1840s.

questions.q: What is the grumpy main character's name in Charles Dickens' 'A Christmas Carol'?

source.notes: Published 1843. His full name: Ebenezer Scrooge. Origin of 'Bah, humbug!'

questions.q: What is the name of the clever 8-year-old in the 1990 film 'Home Alone'?

source.notes: Played by Macaulay Culkin. Directed by Chris Columbus.

questions.q: What is the name of Santa's famous reindeer with a glowing red nose?

source.notes: Created 1939 by Robert L. May for Montgomery Ward. Song by Gene Autry (1949).

questions.q: Which song, performed by Gemini 6 astronauts in 1965, became the first song ever played in space?

source.notes: December 16, 1965. Wally Schirra on harmonica, Tom Stafford on bells.

questions.q: Which red-leaved plant is traditionally associated with Christmas in the United States?

source.notes: Native to Mexico. Introduced to the US by Joel Roberts Poinsett in 1825.

questions.q: Which fairy tale inspired the tradition of decorating gingerbread houses?

source.notes: Brothers Grimm, 1812. House made of bread and candy.

questions.q: Finish the line: 'You better watch out, you better not cry, you better not ___, I'm telling you why.'

source.notes: 'Santa Claus Is Comin' to Town.' Written 1934. First performed by Eddie Cantor.

questions.q: How many reindeer pull Santa's sleigh in the poem 'A Visit from St. Nicholas' (aka 'The Night Before Christmas')?

source.notes: Dasher, Dancer, Prancer, Vixen, Comet, Cupid, Donner, Blitzen. Rudolph was added in 1939.

questions.q: Bing Crosby's recording of which song is the best-selling single of all time?

source.notes: Written by Irving Berlin. Over 50 million copies sold. From the 1942 film 'Holiday Inn.'

questions.q: In 'A Christmas Carol,' how many ghosts visit Scrooge on Christmas Eve?

source.notes: Jacob Marley plus Past, Present, and Yet to Come = 4 total, but only 3 are 'Christmas' ghosts.

questions.q: Which Dr. Seuss character is famous for trying to steal Christmas from the Whos in Whoville?

source.notes: 'How the Grinch Stole Christmas!' published 1957.

questions.q: Which classic 1946 film features George Bailey contemplating suicide before being shown how the world would be without him?

source.notes: Directed by Frank Capra. Starring James Stewart as George Bailey.

questions.q: In the song 'The Twelve Days of Christmas,' what is given on the first day?
choices: A partridge in a pear tree

source.notes: English carol, first printed 1780. Cumulative song structure.

questions.q: Traditionally, on which date do Italians and some other cultures celebrate Epiphany, ending the Christmas season?

source.notes: In Italian tradition, la Befana (witch figure) delivers gifts on this night.

questions.q: Which Christmas figure, according to European folklore, rides a broomstick to deliver gifts on Epiphany?

source.notes: Italian folklore. Often depicted as an old woman with a broomstick.

questions.q: Which iconic New York tree is lit each year at Rockefeller Center, a tradition since the Great Depression?

source.notes: Unofficial tradition began in 1931 among workers; official Rockefeller tree since 1933.

questions.q: 'Frosty the Snowman' was first released as a song in which year?

source.notes: Recorded by Gene Autry. The Rankin/Bass animated TV special came in 1969.

questions.q: What is the name of the musical instrument played by Italian zampognari (traditional Christmas folk musicians)?

source.notes: Italian bagpipes traditionally played during the Novena before Christmas.

questions.q: In 'How the Grinch Stole Christmas!,' the Grinch has a dog named what?

source.notes: Max has one antler tied to his head to disguise him as a reindeer.
